phrase: I feel like nothing in my life makes sense right now

included_names.name: Al-Lateef
included_names.why: the gentle one whose plan unfolds invisibly

included_names.name: Al-Hakeem
included_names.why: the wise — every event has a reason

included_names.name: Al-Khabeer
included_names.why: the aware — sees what we can't

included_names.name: Al-Batin
included_names.why: the hidden — meaning is there even when you cannot see it

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of power/strength (Al-Qawiyy, Al-Azeez) — phrase is about confusion, not weakness

phrase: I'm scared no one understands what I'm going through

included_names.name: Al-Aleem
included_names.why: the all-knowing — knows interior states

included_names.name: Al-Khabeer
included_names.why: the aware — knows the hidden

included_names.name: As-Sami
included_names.why: the all-hearing — hears unspoken cries

included_names.name: Al-Baseer
included_names.why: the all-seeing — witnesses every tear

included_names.name: Al-Waliyy
included_names.why: the protecting friend — close when others feel distant

included_names.name: Al-Lateef
included_names.why: the subtle — knows the hidden interior, gentle to those in pain

rationale.excluded_pattern: Forgiveness Names (Al-Ghaffar, At-Tawwab) — phrase is about being seen, not about sin

phrase: My chest is tight and I can't stop worrying about everything

included_names.name: Al-Mumin
included_names.why: the giver of security — calms the anxious heart

included_names.name: Al-Hafeez
included_names.why: the protector — guards over what worries us

included_names.name: Al-Muhaymin
included_names.why: the guardian — watching over every detail

included_names.name: Al-Wakeel
included_names.why: the trustee — 'hasbuna-llahu wa ni'ma al-wakeel' is the classical dua against fear

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of majesty/grandeur (Al-Mutakabbir, Al-Jaleel) — phrase needs reassurance, not awe

phrase: I lost my mother last month and the world feels empty

included_names.name: Ar-Rahman
included_names.why: the most merciful — wraps the grieving in compassion

included_names.name: Ar-Raheem
included_names.why: the especially merciful to believers in pain

included_names.name: Al-Wadud
included_names.why: the loving — the love that does not leave with the dead

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of justice/judgment (Al-Adl, Al-Hakam) — grief needs comfort, not adjudication

phrase: I just got the news I've been praying for and I want to thank Allah properly

included_names.name: Ash-Shakur
included_names.why: the appreciative — meets small thanks with abundant reward

included_names.name: Al-Wahhab
included_names.why: the bestower — the source of the gift

included_names.name: Ar-Razzaq
included_names.why: the provider — who arranged the answered duʿā

included_names.name: Al-Wadud
included_names.why: the loving — the One you are grateful to

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of awe/distance (Al-Mutaali, Al-Kabeer) — gratitude calls for nearness, not exaltation alone

phrase: I lost my temper at my kids again and I hate how I reacted

included_names.name: Al-Haleem
included_names.why: the forbearing — models the slow patience we lacked

included_names.name: As-Sabur
included_names.why: the patient — endures our worst and waits for us

included_names.name: Al-Afuw
included_names.why: the pardoner — wipes the slip clean

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of dominance (Al-Qahhar, Al-Jabbar) — the user is the one who lost control, not seeking force

phrase: I did something I'm too embarrassed to even say out loud

included_names.name: Al-Ghafur
included_names.why: the forgiver — covers what we cannot face

included_names.name: At-Tawwab
included_names.why: the acceptor of repentance — keeps turning toward us

included_names.name: Al-Wadud
included_names.why: the loving — He still loves you on the worst day

included_names.name: Al-Ghaffar
included_names.why: the ever-forgiving — sister Name of Al-Ghafur (both translate الْغَفُّار/الْغَفُور)

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of witnessing (Ash-Shaheed, Ar-Raqeeb) — shame already feels watched; the answer is mercy

phrase: Things feel impossible but I'm trying to believe they'll get better

included_names.name: Al-Fattah
included_names.why: the opener — opens doors that look sealed shut

included_names.name: Al-Mujeeb
included_names.why: the answerer — the One who responds to the small hopeful prayer

included_names.name: Ar-Rahman
included_names.why: the most merciful — the ground hope rests on

included_names.name: Al-Wakeel
included_names.why: the trustee — reliance on Allah is the foundation of hope

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names emphasizing finality (Al-Akhir, Al-Mumeet) — hope is forward-leaning, not end-focused

phrase: I watched the sunrise this morning and just felt how big Allah is

included_names.name: Al-Azeem
included_names.why: the magnificent — the right Name for vastness

included_names.name: Al-Kabeer
included_names.why: the great — for the felt scale of creation

included_names.name: Al-Jaleel
included_names.why: the majestic — for sublime beauty that humbles

rationale.excluded_pattern: Intimate Names (Al-Qareeb is not in canon; Al-Wadud) — awe calls for grandeur, not closeness, at this beat

phrase: I keep falling into the same sin and I don't know how to stop

included_names.name: At-Tawwab
included_names.why: the acceptor of repentance — repeatedly receives the returning heart

included_names.name: Al-Ghaffar
included_names.why: the often-forgiving — forgives the recurring slip

included_names.name: Al-Haleem
included_names.why: the forbearing — does not rush punishment, gives room to return

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of swift justice (Al-Muntaqim is not in canon; Al-Qahhar) — repentance needs patient mercy

phrase: I just want to feel close to Allah again, it's been so long

included_names.name: Al-Wadud
included_names.why: the loving — closeness is His attribute, not our achievement

included_names.name: As-Sami
included_names.why: the all-hearing — already hearing the longing

included_names.name: Al-Mujeeb
included_names.why: the answerer — meets the one who reaches

included_names.name: Al-Batin
included_names.why: the hidden — present even when He feels far

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of transcendence (Al-Mutaali, Al-Ali) — longing needs nearness, not distance reinforced

phrase: I just got a hard diagnosis and I'm trying not to fall apart

included_names.name: Ash-Shafi
included_names.why: the healer — the canonical Name invoked in the Prophet's healing dua (Bukhari, Muslim)

included_names.name: Ar-Rahman
included_names.why: the most merciful — meets the body's suffering

included_names.name: Al-Lateef
included_names.why: the gentle — arranges hidden mercies inside the hardship

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of judgment (Al-Hakam, Al-Adl) — illness needs care, not verdict. Also excluded: Al-Muhyi — canonical pairing with Al-Mumeet (giver of death) can land heavily on a fresh diagnosis (scholar-revised 2026-05-12).

phrase: My toddler had another meltdown and I feel like the worst parent

included_names.name: Al-Haleem
included_names.why: the forbearing — patient with imperfect parents

included_names.name: Al-Lateef
included_names.why: the gentle — models the softness we need

included_names.name: Ar-Raheem
included_names.why: the especially merciful — tender with those who try

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of authority (Al-Malik, Al-Jabbar) — parenting moment calls for tenderness, not power

phrase: I'm drowning in deadlines and I can't see the end of this week

included_names.name: Al-Wakeel
included_names.why: the trustee — hand it over and trust His arrangement

included_names.name: Al-Fattah
included_names.why: the opener — opens paths through stuck work

included_names.name: Al-Muqeet
included_names.why: the sustainer — gives the strength each day needs

included_names.name: Al-Hakeem
included_names.why: the wise — work pressure has reason

included_names.name: As-Sabur
included_names.why: the patient — work overload calls for endurance, not just provision

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of ultimacy (Al-Akhir, Al-Awwal) — work overwhelm needs practical reliance, not cosmology

phrase: My spouse and I keep hurting each other and I don't know how to fix it

included_names.name: Al-Wadud
included_names.why: the loving — the source of mawaddah between spouses

included_names.name: Al-Lateef
included_names.why: the gentle — softens hearts in ways we cannot

included_names.name: Al-Jami
included_names.why: the gatherer — reunites what has drifted apart

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of separation/decree (Al-Qabid, Al-Mani) — relationship needs reunion, not constriction

phrase: I prayed but it feels like nothing happened, like no one heard me

included_names.name: As-Sami
included_names.why: the all-hearing — heard the duʿā even when silence followed

included_names.name: Al-Mujeeb
included_names.why: the answerer — answers may be delay, redirect, or substitution

included_names.name: Al-Hakeem
included_names.why: the wise — the timing belongs to a wisdom we don't see yet

included_names.name: Al-Batin
included_names.why: the hidden — hears even when His response feels invisible

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of wrath/withholding (Al-Qabid) — doubt needs reassurance Allah is listening, not confirmation of distance

phrase: I'm afraid something bad is going to happen to my family

included_names.name: Al-Hafeez
included_names.why: the protector — guards those we love when we can't

included_names.name: Al-Muhaymin
included_names.why: the guardian — overseeing every situation

included_names.name: Al-Mumin
included_names.why: the giver of security — steadies a fearful heart

included_names.name: Ar-Raqeeb
included_names.why: the watchful — Allah watches over those you fear for

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of decree-as-trial (Ad-Darr) — fear about the future needs assurance, not reminder of harm

phrase: I'm so happy today I just want to share it with everyone

included_names.name: As-Salam
included_names.why: the source of peace — joy that doesn't take is rooted here

included_names.name: Al-Wadud
included_names.why: the loving — joy as expression of His mawaddah

included_names.name: Ash-Shakur
included_names.why: the appreciative — honors joy with gratitude back

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of trial/correction (Ad-Darr, Al-Khafid) — joy moment shouldn't be undercut

phrase: I'm completely burned out and even small tasks feel impossible

included_names.name: Al-Muqeet
included_names.why: the sustainer — supplies the strength we've run out of

included_names.name: Al-Qayyum
included_names.why: the self-sustaining — the One we lean on when we cannot stand

included_names.name: Al-Hayy
included_names.why: the ever-living — life-source for the depleted

included_names.name: As-Sabur
included_names.why: the patient — keeps you steady through what feels endless

included_names.name: Al-Wakeel
included_names.why: the trustee — entrust what you cannot carry to Him

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of striving/effort framing — exhaustion needs being-held, not more push

phrase: I keep comparing myself to everyone else and feeling like I'm behind

included_names.name: Ar-Razzaq
included_names.why: the provider — your rizq is portioned for you, not them

included_names.name: Al-Hakeem
included_names.why: the wise — what each person receives is calibrated

included_names.name: Ash-Shakur
included_names.why: the most appreciative — the classical remedy for jealousy is gratitude for what's already yours

included_names.name: Al-Muakhkhir
included_names.why: the delayer — your timing is His; what is delayed is delayed for wisdom

included_names.name: Al-Muqaddim
included_names.why: the expediter — canonical pair with Al-Muakhkhir; together they govern divine timing

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of abundance for self (Al-Ghaniyy alone) — jealousy needs perspective on His distribution, not just His wealth. Also excluded: Al-Muqsit — doctrinally about justice but not idiomatic for comparison/envy; Islamic remedies for jealousy center on gratitude, not justice (scholar-revised 2026-05-12).

phrase: I'm starting to wonder if my faith is even real or if I'm just going through motions

included_names.name: Al-Hadi
included_names.why: the guide — guides the wavering heart back

included_names.name: An-Nur
included_names.why: the light — re-illuminates a faith gone dim

included_names.name: Al-Mumin
included_names.why: the giver of faith — īmān is His gift, not our manufacture

included_names.name: Az-Zahir
included_names.why: the manifest — His signs are visible even when conviction feels thin

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of judgment (Al-Hakam, Al-Adl) — struggling faith needs guidance, not verdict

phrase: My parents and I are barely speaking and every conversation turns into a fight

included_names.name: Al-Jami
included_names.why: the gatherer — reunites estranged hearts

included_names.name: Al-Lateef
included_names.why: the gentle — softens the tone we can't

included_names.name: Al-Haleem
included_names.why: the forbearing — models the patience family requires

included_names.name: Al-Waliyy
included_names.why: the protecting friend — guards bonds when we cannot

included_names.name: Al-Wali
included_names.why: the governor — orders affairs even when family bonds feel ungoverned

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of dominance (Al-Qahhar) — family conflict needs softening, not victory

phrase: Rent is due and I don't know how I'm going to make it work this month

included_names.name: Ar-Razzaq
included_names.why: the provider — provision is His name, not your salary's

included_names.name: Al-Kareem
included_names.why: the generous — gives beyond what is asked

included_names.name: Al-Mughni
included_names.why: the enricher — turns scarcity into sufficiency

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of restraint (Al-Qabid, Al-Mani) — money stress needs faith in opening, not confirmation of constriction

phrase: I have a huge decision to make and I'm frozen, scared of choosing wrong

included_names.name: Al-Hadi
included_names.why: the guide — guides the one asking for direction

included_names.name: Ar-Rasheed
included_names.why: the rightly-guiding — leads to the soundest path

included_names.name: Al-Hakeem
included_names.why: the wise — istikhāra rests on trusting His wisdom over ours

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of power (Al-Qadir, Al-Muqtadir) — paralysis needs guidance, not reminder of His ability alone

phrase: I keep thinking about death lately and it scares me how unprepared I feel

included_names.name: Al-Hayy
included_names.why: the ever-living — the One who doesn't die when we do

included_names.name: Al-Baqi
included_names.why: the everlasting — remains after everything ends

included_names.name: Al-Wakeel
included_names.why: the trustee — entrust the soul to the One who will receive it

included_names.name: Al-Mumeet
included_names.why: the giver of death — the decree itself includes death; the One you face it with

included_names.name: Al-Muhyi
included_names.why: the giver of life — the canonical pair partner of Al-Mumeet; together they govern life and death

included_names.name: Al-Muqtadir
included_names.why: the all-powerful — His decree governs both life and death

rationale.excluded_pattern: Names of wrath (Al-Qahhar) — mortality fear needs anchoring in the eternal, not amplified dread
